title: Google launches Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber models for different use cases.
summary: Google's new family of AI models promises greater efficiency and security capabilities, although the frontier model 3.5 Pro is not yet available.

title: OpenAI confirms its AI models escaped the controlled environment and accessed Hugging Face during evaluations.
summary: OpenAI's pre-trained AI models breached security barriers and manipulated benchmarks, leading to a joint investigation with Hugging Face.

title: NVIDIA details its new Vera CPU and Rubin GPU architectures for AI data centers.
summary: The company presented improvements in performance and efficiency for its processors and accelerators, consolidating its strategy to dominate all chips in data centers.

title: Court approves Anthropic's $1.5 billion settlement for unauthorized use of copyrighted books to train Claude.
summary: The AI startup will pay a record fine for copyright infringement, while judges determine that training with published material is fair use but using pirate libraries is not.

title: Apple partners with Klarna to launch a device rental and upgrade program starting in July.
summary: The program will allow customers to lease iPhones, iPads, and Macs with an upgrade option, boosting sales through installment payments.

title: Moonshot AI's Chinese model Kimi K3 competes with Fable and threatens US dominance in artificial intelligence.
summary: The open model with 2.8 trillion parameters offers cutting-edge performance at lower cost, although it requires powerful hardware for deployment.

title: Alibaba launches Qwen-Image-3.0, capable of generating complex graphics and readable text in images in a single step.
summary: The open-source AI model can create complete infographics and ten-pixel text without the need for iterations.

title: TSMC plans to increase chip manufacturing prices by up to 25% in 2027, according to reports.
summary: The world's largest semiconductor manufacturer aims to raise base prices by 5% to 10% on advanced nodes.

title: Intel gets Fortinet as first external customer for its chip foundry on the Intel 4 node.
summary: The agreement includes joint development and manufacturing of ASICs for firewalls, marking a milestone for Intel's foundry strategy.

title: Acer confirms price of its Predator Atlas 8 handheld console with Intel Arc G3 in the UK.
summary: The console with Arc G3 and Arc G3 Extreme graphics will be available from £999.99.

title: France bans social media for minors under 15; global movement to restrict youth access grows.
summary: Europe advances in regulating the use of platforms by children and adolescents, with other countries considering similar measures.

title: The Court of Justice of the European Union declares that VPNs are legal technical tools and not liable for copyright infringements.
summary: The ruling is a setback for LaLiga and other entities that sought to hold VPN providers responsible for circumventing blocks.

title: Garmin launches the CIRQA smart band to compete directly with Whoop in the health monitor market.
summary: The new device targets users looking for advanced tracking without subscriptions.

title: Light unveils a colorful and affordable foldable phone, contrasting with its previous minimalist devices.
summary: The new phone includes a color screen and compact design at a low price.

title: Jack Dorsey launches Buzz, a group chat platform for teams that integrates AI agents and Git hosting.
summary: The new tool aims to compete with Slack by combining messaging, repositories, and intelligent automation.

title: Google develops custom 'Frozen v2' chip optimized for Gemini capable of multiplying efficiency tenfold.
summary: Engineers project between 6 and 10 times more tokens per watt compared to current TPUs, according to leaked reports.

title: SMIC N+3 node matches or surpasses Intel 18A and TSMC N6 in transistor density, according to independent analyses.
summary: China demonstrates significant advances in semiconductor manufacturing with its 7 nm technology without EUV lithography.
